item.title: ‘Drug dealer’ held with 3.90kg heroin in Sirajganj
item.description: Members of Rapid Action Battalion arrested an alleged drug dealer with 3.90 kilograms of heroin during a special anti-drug operation in Sirajgan on Sunday night....

item.title: Juba Dal leader stabbed in Chuadanga
item.description: A LOCAL leader of Juba Dal, the youth wing of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, was stabbed allegedly by supporters of the Awami League, activities of which are banned for July atrocity trials, near a mosque in Iswarchandrapur village of Darshana upazila in Kushtia on Sunday night....Political party merchandise

item.title: Rangpur farmers happy as mango trees bloom
item.description: A bumper harvest of the popular fruit is primarily predicted for the current season as mango trees bloom profusely, delighting farmers in all five districts of the Rangpur agricultural region....

item.title: 2 JCD factions clash at GSTU, 2 injured
item.description: Two factions of the Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal clashed repeatedly at Gopalganj Science and Technology University on Sunday over the appointment of a new proctor, leaving two leaders injured....

item.title: 2,701 constables to be recruited on emergency basis: home minister
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Monday said that 2,701 police constables would be recruited on an emergency basis to address the existing manpower shortage in the force....

item.title: HUMAN HAULER DRIVER MURDER: RAB arrests 3 accused
item.description: Members of Rapid Action Battalion have arrested three accused in connection with the killing of a human hauler driver in the capital’s Jatrabari area....

item.title: Publishers fear fewer new titles due to short duration
item.description: The Amar Ekushey Book Fair 2026, scheduled to be inaugurated on Thursday, is likely to see a smaller number of new titles due to its shorter duration this year....

item.title: PM attends office at Armed Forces Division for first time
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Monday attended his office at the Armed Forces Division at the Dhaka Cantonment in the capital for the first time....Public speaking training

item.title: Ahmed Sharif’s death anniv today
item.description: Today is the 27th anniversary of the death of educationist, philosopher, critic, writer and scholar of medieval Bangla literature Ahmed Sharif....

item.title: Bangla Academy names winners of literary award 2025
item.description: The Bangla Academy on Monday announced the names of the winners of its literary award 2025....

item.title: First JS session summoned on March 12
item.description: President Mohammed Shahabuddin on Monday summoned the first session of the 13th Jatiya Sangsad at 11:00am on March 12 in the Parliament House at Sher-e-Bangla Nagar in Dhaka....

item.title: HC orders closure of abandoned tube-well boreholes
item.description: The High Court on Monday directed authorities to fill up open boreholes of abandoned deep tube-wells across the country to prevent children from falling into them and dying....

item.title: Jamaat opposes BNP leaders’ appointment to city corporations
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Monday opposed the government decision of appointing BNP leaders in six city corporations....Cultural exchange programs

item.title: PCJSS member injured in ‘gunfight’ with army
item.description: A member of the Parbatya Chattagram Jana Samhati Samiti was found bullet-hit after a reported gunfight with the members of the Bangladesh Army in the Murang Bazar area under Rowangchhari upazila in Bandarban Monday afternoon.,,...

item.title: Smart farmer card pilot to be rolled out soon
item.description: The government is moving to introduce a smart ‘farmer card’ aimed at integrating cultivators into a comprehensive, technology-driven support framework, with officials working to launch a pilot project as soon as preparations are completed....

item.title: Iran says would respond ‘ferociously’ to any US attack
item.description: Iran warned the United States on Monday that any attack would be met ‘ferociously’ in response to president Donald Trump’s threat of limited strikes, while students staged new protests against the Islamic republic....

item.title: HC orders probe into Ibn Sina job circular
item.description: The High Court on Monday asked the government to investigate a ‘discriminatory’ job circular and ensure withdrawal of the ‘biased’ conditions in the circular....Political party merchandise

item.title: Govt to form another commission to investigate 2009 BDR carnage
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Monday said that the government would form a commission to reinvestigate the 2009 carnage at Pilkhana, the headquarters of the erstwhile Bangladesh Rifles now known as Border Guard Bangladesh, in Dhaka....

item.title: Profit, price hike and adulteration
item.description: RAMADAN is meant to be a time of spiritual purification, empathy, restraint and compassion. It is a period when the Muslims reflect on their responsibilities towards the less fortunate and strengthen social bonds through charity and generosity. Unfortunately, the reality unfolding in this month often contradicts these ideals. Instead of fostering solidarity and relief......

item.title: Aggrandising theatre and impunity of genocide
item.description: MORE than 75,000 Palestinians have been murdered in Gaza, with tens of thousands more still missing beneath the rubble. They did not die to ‘build a home’ or privatize their beaches. They had homes and free beaches. Gaza was a living city — albeit under decades of Israeli siege — before it was levelled by the most advanced global terror machine on earth, armed......

item.title: The Inquilab–Azadi debate
item.description: LANGUAGE is not merely a means of communication; it is the bearer of a society’s experience, history, culture, politics and consciousness. Therefore, the members of a linguistic community do not see their language merely as an instrument of expression — they hold deep emotion and respect for it. Because of this respect and emotion, struggles for language......

item.title: Decentralising medical education the right way
item.description: THE promise of a medical college in every district of Bangladesh is a seductive one. It carries the weight of ‘development delivered’, appearing in the public imagination as a symbol of progress, regional justice, and a humane response to a desperate need. For the rural family, it means a doctor close to home; for the middle-class student, it is a ladder of upward......Cultural exchange programs

item.title: Deepening democracy through liberating education
item.description: EDUCATION plays a vital role in cultivating democratic values and civic responsibility, helping to build a just and inclusive society. Schools bring together children from diverse socio-economic and cultural backgrounds and therefore hold a unique potential to foster equality and a shared sense of belonging. John Dewey, in his book Democracy and Education, argued......

item.title: Enforcement needed to uphold public interest
item.description: THE obstruction of lawful market oversight drive by traders is a troubling episode that deserves unequivocal condemnation. Market monitoring is not a punitive exercise. It is an essential administrative tool to ensure fair price, curb market manipulation and protect the interests of consumers, especially the low- and middle-income people who feel the pinch of price volatility......

item.title: Legal harassment of political opposition must end
item.description: LEGAL harassment of opposition leaders and dissenting voices was one of the common methods that the deposed Awami League regime used to repress the public. Arbitrary detention in politically motivated cases often led to months, and even years, of incarceration for supporters of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami and other political parties......Political party merchandise

item.title: Super-sub Sesko fires United to win at Everton
item.description:
Benjamin Sesko came off the bench to fire Manchester United up to fourth in the Premier League with a 1-0 win at Everton to extend Michael Carrick's unbeaten start in charge of the Red Devils.
...

item.title: West Indies thrash Zimbabwe at T20 World Cup
item.description:
The West Indies blew away T20 World Cup surprise packages Zimbabwe by a colossal 107 runs in Mumbai on Monday as Shai Hope's team opened their Super Eights campaign with a dominant victory.
...

item.title: Israeli settlers attack West Bank mosque
item.description: Israeli settlers attempted to set fire to a mosque in the occupied West Bank village of Tell early Monday, the Palestinian Authority’s ministry of religious affairs said....

item.title: Russian overnight strikes across Ukraine kill 3
item.description: Russian strikes killed three people and wounded several others overnight, regional authorities said Monday, as Moscow continued its assaults on the eve of the war’s four‑year anniversary....

item.title: India says key Maoist leader surrenders as net tightens
item.description: Indian government officials have said the surrender of a senior Maoist guerrilla commander heralds the ‘final phase’ to end the decades-long Maoist insurgency....

item.title: NY orders citywide travel ban as major storm hits US
item.description: New York ordered drivers off the road and shut down schools on Monday, while residents hunkered down for a massive snowstorm hitting the United States northeast....

item.title: Hanging body of househelp recovered at BGB quarter
item.description: Police recovered the hanging body of a minor househelp from a house in Border Guard Bangladesh Headquarters on Monday afternoon....Cultural exchange programs

item.title: DNC sues three for amassing assets thru drug trafficking
item.description: The Department of Narcotics Control has filed two money laundering cases in Rajshahi against three individuals, accusing them of accumulating crores of taka allegedly through drug trafficking and legalising that through property purchases and financial transactions....

item.title: Recommended officers of 45th BCS asked to submit FF certificates
item.description: The government on Monday asked the recommended candidates to submit freedom fighter certificates under the FF quota for appointment to various cadre posts through the 45th Bangladesh Civil Service....

item.title: Committed to working with SAARC countries to achieve peace, prosperity: Shama
item.description: State minister for foreign affairs Shama Obaed Islam has reaffirmed Bangladesh’s unwavering support for SAARC process and its commitment to working with member states to achieve the shared vision of peace, stability and prosperity in South Asia....

item.title: Labour ministry adopts 22 plans for execution in 180 days
item.description: The Ministry of Labour and Employment on Monday adopted 22 action plans to be implemented within 180 days of the government taking office, according to a press release....

item.title: JnU Career Club unveils new leadership, launches website
item.description: Jagannath University Career Club marked a leadership transition on Monday with the formal handover of responsibilities to its newly elected presidential panel and the launch of its first official website, jnucc.org....
